summary: Simplified app to focus on basic habit tracking, then synthesized a trading strategy from Brooks' transcripts. Dominant thread: reducing complexity.
lessons: Simple habits beat complex AI for user retention.
Delegating research cuts analysis time significantly.
Mechanical strategies work better than ML for backtesting.
Focus on one problem at a time to avoid context switching.
Document synthesis reveals patterns faster than raw data.

items.title: Check files.zip for next task
items.body: I was trying to simplify the app to focus on simple habit tracking like drinking water instead of complex AI-generated plans.
decisions: Drop AI plan generation for complex goals
Remove multi-variant sampling and web search grounding
open: How to handle plan upload and calendar integration
Whether to keep any AI features for parsing simple habits
Native app requirement for iOS reminders

items.title: Fetch yage newsletter
items.body: I was trying to fetch the latest newsletter from yage-ai.kit.com and save it to my SOP folder.
decisions: Ran the fetch script as specified
Script successfully discovered and downloaded new posts

items.title: Extract VTT transcripts and analyze trading strategy
items.body: I was trying to extract VTT transcripts from Brooks price action and synthesize his trading strategy, then evaluate if it could be programmed and backtested.
decisions: Delegated research agent to read 29 English transcripts across 7 modules
Synthesized strategy into comprehensive markdown document covering core philosophy, market states, bar reading, MTR, pullbacks, ranges, breakouts, risk management, and order flow
Decided a meaningful subset could be backtested as rule-encoded approximation
Chose mechanical approach with Python/backtrader over ML or hybrid methods
Built v1 strong breakout strategy with trend bar detection, entry rules, and risk management
open: Refine stop placement to reduce oversized risk per trade
Implement MTR and pullback strategies after v1 validation

decisions: Drop AI plan generation for simplicity
Focus on repeatable habits over complex goals
Choose mechanical over ML backtesting approach
Build breakout strategy as v1 approximation
open_threads: Refine stop placement to reduce risk
Decide on native app for iOS reminders
